title: barringtonia macrophylla
intro: Barringtonia macrophylla, also known as the sea poison or poison tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lecythidaceae, native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been found to possess various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, which have been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues. The health benefits of Barringtonia macrophylla include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine as a topical application to treat various ailments.
